q: Yesterday we ____ a new poem in our English lesson. (learn)
My sister ____ all her homework before dinner last night. (finish)
The teacher ____ us an interesting story about animals. (tell)
We ____ to school by bus this morning because it was raining. (go)
I ____ my pencil case at home, so I had to borrow a pen. (leave)
Please be quiet, the teacher ____ writing on the whiteboard. (be)
Look! The pupils ____ reading their books in the library now. (be)
I can't talk right now because I am ____ for my maths test. (study)
Our class has just ____ the new science project. (begin)
I have ____ all the answers in my exercise book. (write)
The teacher ____ already marked our spelling tests this week.
Maths is ____ than history for me, but I still get good marks. (difficult)
This question is ____ than the last one, so I finished it quickly. (easy)
Anna is the ____ pupil in our whole class. (clever)
Next week we are ____ to visit the science museum with our teacher. (go)
Don't worry about the exam, I'm sure you ____ pass it easily.
How ____ books did you borrow from the school library?
I haven't got ____ time, so I must hurry to my next lesson.
There are only a ____ pupils absent today, just two or three.
Our English class always starts ____ nine o'clock in the morning.
We have a big exam ____ Monday, so I'm studying tonight.
This blue pen isn't yours, it's ____. I bought it yesterday.
My ruler is broken, so can I please use ____? Yours looks new.
You ____ do your homework before you watch television tonight.
If you don't understand the lesson, you ____ ask the teacher for help.
I studied very hard, ____ I passed the difficult exam.
She likes maths ____ she doesn't like history very much.

The lessons about numbers, adding and counting are called ____.
At the end of term we take a big test called an ____ to check what we learned.
Last weekend we ____ to the cinema and saw an exciting new film. (go)
My brother ____ his favourite computer game for three hours yesterday. (play)
I ____ a brilliant new book at the library last Saturday. (find)
She ____ photos of the mountains when she went hiking last summer. (take)
We ____ a sandcastle on the beach during our holiday. (build)
Be quiet, please. Tom is ____ to his favourite music in his room. (listen)
Look at the girls in the park. They are ____ their bikes very fast. (ride)
I can't come out now because I ____ painting a picture for art class. (be)
Sara has ____ a medal in the swimming competition this year. (win)
I have just ____ a beautiful song on my new guitar. (sing)
My parents ____ never tried rock climbing before today.
Chess is ____ interesting than draughts, in my opinion. (interesting)
Skateboarding is the ____ exciting hobby I have ever tried. (exciting)
Reading comics is ____ fun than doing homework. (fun)
My grandparents are ____ to visit us next weekend to watch our dance show. (go)
I think our team ____ win the football match on Sunday because we have practised a lot.
There aren't ____ free seats left at the concert tonight.
I don't have ____ time to finish my puzzle before dinner.
Would you like ____ popcorn while we watch the cartoon?
My swimming lesson always starts ____ nine o'clock in the morning.
We usually go cycling together ____ Saturdays in the summer.
This new skateboard isn't mine, I think it is ____.
Anna lost her tennis racket, so she borrowed ____ from her friend Ben.
You ____ wear a helmet when you ride your bike to stay safe.
If you want to join the chess club, you ____ to sign up before Friday.
I wanted to go skating, ____ it was raining all afternoon.
Liam loves music, ____ he joined the school band this year.
A person who collects stamps as a hobby is called a stamp ____.
The group of people who watch a film or a show at the theatre is called the ____.
A board game with black and white pieces where you try to capture the king is called ____.
Yesterday I ____ a new pair of shoes at the shopping centre. (buy)
She ____ her old bike for fifty thousand togrog last week. (sell)
We ____ to the market on Saturday and got some fresh fruit. (go)
The shop assistant ____ me the price of the jacket. (tell)
I ____ for my drink with a ten thousand togrog note. (pay)
Look, the customers ____ at the checkout right now. (wait)
My mum ____ a shopping list in the kitchen at the moment. (write)
Be quiet, the cashier ____ to another customer. (talk)
I have already ____ all my pocket money this month. (spend)
She ____ just bought a beautiful new dress from that shop.
We have ____ a great bargain in the sale today. (find)
This shirt is ____ than the one I bought last year. (cheap)
The gold ring was the ____ thing in the whole shop. (expensive)
Online shopping is much ____ than going to the mall. (easy)
Tomorrow I am ____ to buy a present for my best friend. (go)
If you give me the money, I ____ pay the bill for you.
How ____ money do you have left in your wallet?
There are too ____ people in the queue today.
I only have a ____ coins, so I cannot buy the toy.
The new shopping centre opens ____ Monday morning.
The shops usually close ____ nine o'clock in the evening.
This wallet is not yours, it is ____ because I bought it.
Those bags belong to my parents, so they are ____.
You ____ pay before you leave the shop with the goods.
If you want to save money, you ____ compare the prices first.
I wanted the red coat, ____ it was too expensive for me.
The sale had finished, ____ we could not buy anything cheap.
The person who takes your money at the shop and sits at the till is the ____.
A small piece of paper that shows what you bought and how much you paid is called a ____.
When a shop sells things at a lower price for a short time, we call it a ____.
Last summer my brother ____ a fireman after years of training. (become)
The pilot ____ the plane safely to London yesterday. (fly)
My aunt ____ science at our school for the first time last week. (teach)
The builders ____ the new hospital two months ago. (finish)
When he was young, my grandad ____ as a farmer in the countryside. (work)
Look! The chef is ____ a huge meal for the guests right now. (cook)
My sister and I ____ writing letters about the jobs we want to do. (be)
The nurse is ____ the doctor in the hospital at the moment. (help)
My cousin has just ____ his first cake as a real baker. (make)
The scientists ____ already finished the important experiment in the lab.
Our teacher has ____ us a project about future jobs. (give)
Being a doctor is ____ than being a shop assistant. (hard)
My dad thinks a vet is the ____ job in the whole world. (good)
For me, helping people is the ____ important part of any job. (important)
When I grow up, I am ____ to be an engineer like my mum. (go)
I think robots ____ do many of our jobs in the future.
A good teacher needs ____ patience to work with young children.
There aren't ____ jobs for astronauts, so very few people become one.
The artist used only a ____ colours to paint the beautiful picture.
Most people in this country start their first job ____ the age of eighteen.
My mother always goes to work early ____ Monday mornings.
That police uniform is not mine; I think it is ____.
Our classroom has a map of jobs, but the one in the next room is bigger than ____.
If you want to be a pilot, you ____ study very hard at maths.
A firefighter ____ be very brave when there is a big fire.
She wanted to be a singer, ____ she practised her songs every single day.
I would love to be a dentist, ____ I am a little afraid of teeth.
A person who flies aeroplanes for a living is called a ____.
The place where a doctor or nurse works to help sick people is a ____.
A person who cooks food in a restaurant kitchen is called a ____.
Yesterday I ____ my homework before dinner and then watched TV. (finish)
She ____ to school by bus last Monday because it was raining. (go)
We ____ a great film at the cinema two days ago. (see)
My brother ____ up very late on Saturday and missed breakfast. (get)
They ____ their grandparents in the countryside last weekend. (visit)
Be quiet, please. Dad is ____ in his bedroom right now. (sleep)
Look! The children ____ their teeth before bed. (brush)
I can't talk now because I ____ dinner for the family. (cook)
I have already ____ my bed, so my room looks tidy. (make)
My sister has ____ all her chores, so she can play outside now. (do)
We ____ never been to London, but we want to go one day.
My morning routine is ____ than my brother's because I wake up at six. (early)
Of all the days, Monday is the ____ day of my week. (busy)
After a long school day, I feel much ____ than in the morning. (tired)
I'm very tired, so I think I ____ go to bed early tonight.
Look at those dark clouds. It is ____ to rain this afternoon. (go)
I don't have ____ free time on weekdays because of my homework.
There are too ____ things on my to-do list this morning.
Would you like ____ milk in your tea before you leave for school?
I always brush my teeth ____ the morning before breakfast.
My family has a big dinner together ____ Sundays.
This is my toothbrush, and the blue one is ____.
My routine is busy, but my sister's is calm, so this messy schedule must be ____.
You look really tired, so you ____ go to bed earlier tonight.
School starts at eight, so I ____ to leave home by half past seven every day.
I woke up late, ____ I missed the school bus this morning.
I wanted to play outside, ____ I had to do my homework first.
The first meal you eat in the morning is called ____.
The set of things you do every day in the same order is called your daily ____.
The small jobs you do at home, like washing dishes or cleaning, are called ____.
